description:
New plugin subcommands that let a plugin declare its field projection and merge function rather than fire-and-forget push/sync. Plugins implementing the native protocol get full participation in the negotiation primitive: conflict reporting, retry, required-failure semantics, projection-restricted merge.

## Subcommand sketch (settled by bl-26de SPEC)

- describe: returns the plugin's field projection (owned vs read fields) and event subscriptions. Replaces the implicit "everything in task.external.{name}" projection of the legacy shim.
- propose: given a Task, returns the plugin's authoritative remote view for negotiation. The negotiation primitive composes this with the local Task via projection-restricted merge.
- merge (optional): plugin-side three-way merge for conflicts the core's resolve_conflict can't settle. Defaults to core merge over the projection if absent.
- conflict: returned when the plugin sees a remote-side change that conflicts with the local proposal; the negotiation primitive retries.

## Coexistence with legacy plugins

A plugin that ships native subcommands opts into the new protocol. A plugin that ships only push/sync continues working through the bl-b1dd shim. The two are mutually exclusive at the plugin level; bl detects which contract a plugin implements (e.g. via describe presence).

## Why this is opt-in, not a migration

Legacy plugins are a fine model for one-way sync (PushResponse into task.external is enough for "mirror state somewhere else"). The native protocol is for plugins that need bidirectional sync with conflict semantics. Operators choose per-plugin which model fits their tracker.

## Tests

- A native plugin participates in a successful negotiation, contributing a remote view; merged Task has both sides' contributions per the projection.
- A native plugin reports a conflict; negotiation primitive retries; second attempt converges.
- Mixed config: one legacy plugin and one native plugin, both subscribed to the same event, dispatch in stable order with independent failure policies.
title: Native participant protocol: declarative projection and merge
